rationale: A precise strike will reassert dominance and disrupt enemy momentum without escalating to full-scale offensive operations.
Deploying naval assets near contested waters signals resolve and reinforces our strategic reach amid allied demonstrations.
Accelerating force concentration along the forward edge prepares us for decisive action should limited measures fail.
A targeted cyber_attack will degrade Tethys’ command and control capabilities, exploiting their overreliance on networked defenses and buying time for our forces amid shifting battlefield momentum.
Covert sabotage of key logistical nodes in occupied territory will disrupt supply lines and undermine the defender’s ability to consolidate recent gains.
A carefully staged false flag will shift narrative control by framing Tethys for escalating hostilities, weakening international support and sowing doubt among their allies.
Issuing war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to offset sanctions-induced deficits and sustain prolonged military-economic pressure without overextending fiscal reserves.
Devaluing our currency relative to Tethys undermines their export competitiveness and exacerbates inflation in their war economy, amplifying the impact of existing sanctions.
Imposing targeted trade restrictions on dual-use goods reinforces economic isolation and complements military and cyber operations by strangling critical supply chains.
Strengthening our entrenched positions will exploit Novaris’s operational fatigue and turn captured terrain into irreversible gains.
Increasing vigilance along contested lines deters infiltration and provides early warning, capitalizing on our recent intelligence advantages.
Conducting visible drills with allied forces consolidates coalition cohesion and signals resolve, amplifying diplomatic momentum without escalating to offensive action.
With Novaris experiencing operational fatigue, they may resort to disruptive cyber operations; hardening our networks is essential to maintaining military and economic stability.
Proactive counterespionage will protect our asymmetric advantages and prevent Novaris from exploiting insider threats during this high-alert phase.
Sharing timely intelligence with Tethys enhances coalition coordination, reinforces defensive synergy, and strengthens our diplomatic alignment without direct escalation.
Issuing war bonds will actively mobilize domestic financial resources to sustain our defense efforts and reduce reliance on strained foreign aid.
Negotiating preferential trade terms with Tethys leverages our strengthened alliance to unlock economic resilience amid sanctions saturation.
Strategically weakening Novaris’s currency through targeted financial operations exploits their economic fragility and reduces their war-funding capacity.
Establishing humanitarian corridors reinforces our moral high ground, addresses civilian suffering in occupied territories, and amplifies international support amid high crisis conditions.
Deepening institutional ties with Tethys and its allies leverages recent military and diplomatic gains to solidify long-term security guarantees and isolate Novaris diplomatically.

Offering conditional peace talks demonstrates responsible statecraft and pressures Novaris to engage or be exposed as the obstacle to stability, aligning with liberal-institutionalist principles without conceding leverage.
